relevant_files.role: Language registration hub: get_language_info() and get_ts_language() dispatch all language queries; LanguageInfo struct defines required fields (name, language, element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, defuse_query, optional handlers)

Template implementation: ELEMENT_QUERY (method_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_declaration), CALL_QUERY (method_invocation), REFERENCE_QUERY (type_identifier), IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY, extract_inheritance handler with superclass/interfaces extraction, unit tests pattern
Extension registry: EXTENSION_MAP array maps file extensions to language identifiers; supported_languages() returns feature-gated slice; both require #[cfg(feature)] guards
Feature flags and dependencies: lang-* features are optional, included in default set; tree-sitter-* crates are optional workspace dependencies
Workspace root: defines workspace.dependencies section with tree-sitter versions (0.26.6) and language grammar versions (e.g., tree-sitter-java 0.23.5)
Integration test patterns: feature-gated tests for language-specific functionality; tests verify symbol extraction, call graph, inheritance
Documentation: Supported Languages table lists all enabled languages with extensions and feature flags; must be updated to include Kotlin

conventions.commits: Conventional commits (feat, fix, etc.), GPG signed, DCO sign-off required; commitlint enforces header max 100 chars, body-leading-blank always, subject-case disabled for bot compatibility
conventions.testing: AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) in unit tests; feature-gated test modules (#[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-*"))]); parse_<lang>() helpers; one happy path and one edge case per behavior; integration tests in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo deny check advisories licenses; CI enforces all three
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries (code-analyze-core); Option/Result types; tree-sitter parsing errors handled via Option unwrapping with fallbacks

patterns: Language support is modular: each language in separate file under crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/, feature-gated in mod.rs
Query constants (ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY) are &'static str; DEFUSE_QUERY is optional (Fortran omits it)
Handler functions (extract_inheritance, extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver) are optional; set to None if language doesn't support the concept
Extension registry in lang.rs uses feature gates to conditionally include extensions; supported_languages() returns feature-gated slice
get_language_info() and get_ts_language() use exhaustive match over all supported languages; both must be updated together
Tree-sitter language objects obtained via tree_sitter_<lang>::LANGUAGE.into() conversion
Unit tests use parse_<lang>() helper to create tree-sitter::Tree; SemanticExtractor for defuse site testing
Inheritance extraction walks AST node children by field name (e.g., superclass, interfaces in Java; delegation_specifiers in Kotlin)

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Swift grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Parallel language addition; Swift also JVM-adjacent (cross-platform); shares similar inheritance/interface patterns

related_issues.title: bench(analyze_symbol): v15 MCP-only def-use micro-benchmark
related_issues.relevance: Benchmarking framework; Kotlin addition will be included in future benchmark runs to measure performance impact

constraints: tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 is the target version (fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin); must be added to workspace.dependencies
Feature flag lang-kotlin must be optional in crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml and included in default feature set
Extensions .kt and .kts must be registered in EXTENSION_MAP behind #[cfg(feature = "lang-kotlin")]
All queries must compile: tree-sitter query syntax is strict; invalid S-expressions cause parse errors at runtime
DEFUSE_QUERY is required (unlike Fortran which omits it); Kotlin supports def-use analysis via identifier nodes
Handlers (extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type) must be None unless Kotlin AST provides the semantic information
Unit tests must pass with cargo test --features lang-kotlin; integration tests must verify symbol extraction works end-to-end
No SPDX header changes; only add new file with Apache-2.0 header
test_coverage: Existing test coverage for language registration: crates/code-analyze-core/tests/integration_tests.rs includes feature-gated tests for each language (symbol extraction, call graph, inheritance). New kotlin.rs module must include: (1) parse_kotlin() helper, (2) test_kotlin_element_query_happy_path (free function, method, class, data class, interface, enum, object), (3) test_kotlin_call_query_happy_path (method and function calls), (4) test_kotlin_extract_inheritance (single superclass with parens, multiple interfaces, mixed delegation), (5) test_kotlin_import_query (import statements), (6) test_defuse_query_write_site (variable declaration and assignment). All tests must be under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-kotlin"))].

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE (Language object); node kinds: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, call_expression, delegation_specifiers, import_header
library_findings.notes: fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin is the canonical Kotlin grammar for tree-sitter. Kotlin class hierarchy uses delegation_specifiers for inheritance: class Foo : Bar(), Baz { }. Walk delegation_specifiers children to extract superclass (has constructor call with parens) vs interfaces (no parens). Companion objects declared as companion object { } inside class; no special extraction needed. Modifiers (data, sealed, inner) are part of class_declaration modifiers node. Imports use import_header node.

library_findings.relevant_api: Query syntax: (node_kind field: (child_kind) @capture) @parent_capture; alternation [a | b]; optional ?; zero-or-more *; one-or-more +. Rust bindings: Parser::new(), set_language(), parse(); Query::new(); QueryCursor; Node traversal via child_by_field_name(), named_child(), kind(), start_byte(), end_byte()
library_findings.notes: Tree-sitter queries are S-expressions matching node kinds and field names. Captures (@name) are extracted by QueryCursor. The crate uses tree-sitter 0.26.6 workspace dependency. All language grammars follow tree-sitter query syntax; Kotlin grammar is no exception.

approaches.name: Approach 1: Minimal Kotlin support (no DEFUSE_QUERY)
approaches.description: Add kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (type_identifier), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header), and extract_inheritance handler. Set defuse_query to None. Register in mod.rs, lang.rs, and Cargo.toml. Justification: Kotlin's identifier node structure is complex due to qualified names and lambda receivers; DEFUSE_QUERY would require extensive testing to avoid false positives. Deferring def-use analysis allows faster initial release and can be added in a follow-up PR with dedicated benchmarking.
pros: Faster implementation: skips DEFUSE_QUERY design and testing
Reduces risk: fewer query patterns to debug
Aligns with Fortran precedent: Fortran also omits defuse_query
Simpler test suite: no def-use site extraction tests needed
cons: Incomplete feature parity: other languages have DEFUSE_QUERY
Limits analysis depth: cannot track variable definitions and uses
May require rework: adding DEFUSE_QUERY later is a breaking change to the module API

approaches.name: Approach 2: Full Kotlin support with DEFUSE_QUERY
approaches.description: Add kotlin.rs with all queries including DEFUSE_QUERY modeled after Java: (local_variable_declaration declarator: (variable_declarator name: (identifier) @write.local)), (assignment_expression left: (identifier) @write.assign), (update_expression (identifier) @writeread.update), (identifier) @read.usage. Kotlin uses similar syntax for variable declarations and assignments. Extract inheritance via delegation_specifiers: walk children to find superclass_type_with_constructor (has parens) vs user_type (interfaces, no parens). Register in mod.rs, lang.rs, Cargo.toml. Include comprehensive unit tests for all query patterns and def-use extraction.
pros: Feature parity: matches Java, Python, TypeScript, Go, C++, C#, JavaScript
Complete analysis: enables def-use tracking for variable lifecycle analysis
Future-proof: no rework needed; aligns with project's analysis depth
Better benchmarking: can measure def-use performance on Kotlin codebases
cons: Higher complexity: DEFUSE_QUERY requires careful node kind mapping
More testing: must verify def-use extraction doesn't produce false positives
Longer implementation: additional time for query validation and test coverage
Kotlin-specific edge cases: qualified names, extension functions, lambda receivers may complicate identifier matching

approaches.name: Approach 3: Phased rollout with feature flag disabled by default
approaches.description: Implement full Kotlin support (Approach 2) but add lang-kotlin as an optional feature NOT included in the default feature set. This allows early adopters to opt-in while the implementation matures. Add a note in CONTRIBUTING.md that lang-kotlin is experimental. Update README to mark Kotlin as 'beta'. In a future release, promote lang-kotlin to the default set once benchmarks confirm performance is acceptable.
pros: Reduces risk: experimental code doesn't affect default builds
Allows iteration: can refine queries based on real-world Kotlin codebases
Transparent versioning: users know lang-kotlin is not yet stable
Easier rollback: can be removed or fixed without breaking existing users
cons: Incomplete default support: users must opt-in to use Kotlin
Maintenance burden: must test both default and experimental builds in CI
Confusing UX: users may not discover Kotlin support if not in default
Defers full integration: doesn't fully resolve the issue until promotion

recommendation: Approach 2 (Full Kotlin support with DEFUSE_QUERY). Rationale: (1) Issue explicitly requests DEFUSE_QUERY in acceptance criteria ('DEFUSE_QUERY' is not mentioned as optional); (2) Feature parity with 9 other supported languages (all have defuse_query); (3) Kotlin's JVM-style AST is similar to Java, reducing risk of query errors; (4) Comprehensive unit tests (specified in issue) will validate correctness; (5) Aligns with project's design philosophy of deep code analysis; (6) No technical blocker: tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 is stable and well-tested. Implementation should follow the Java template closely, adapting node kinds and field names to Kotlin's grammar. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours for queries, handlers, tests, and integration.
